expressions.english: a color which one likes
a person who one likes
a drama which one likes
to like the most
Do you like coffee?
Will the children like it?
expressions.notes:
아이 = child; kid; baby
-(으)를까요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.english: Is there anyone who you like?
Which/What kind of movies do you like?
expressions.notes:
어떤 = what kind of; which
영화 = movie

expressions.english: I like to be alone.
I don't really like hiking.

This is the book I like most.
This is my favorite book.
expressions.notes:
이게 = this (subject)
이게 is the contracted form of 이것이.
제 = (polite) my; I
제일 좋아하는 = one's favorite
책 = book

expressions.korean: 이 옷은 제가 제일 좋아하는 옷이에요.
expressions.english: This is the clothing I like the most.

expressions.korean: 이렇게 하면 손님들이 좋아할 것 같아요.
expressions.english: If we do it like this, I think the customers will like it.
expressions.notes:
하다 = to do
손님 = guest; visitor

expressions.english: Do you want to eat some ice cream? Which flavor do you like?
expressions.notes:
아이스크림 = ice cream
드시다 = (honorific) to eat; to drink
-(으)래요? = Do you want to + V?; Shall we + V?
무슨 = what; what kind of
맛 = taste

expressions.korean: 외국어를 배우는 걸 좋아하는데 잘하지는 못 해요.
expressions.english: I like learning foreign languages, but I am not good at it.
expressions.notes:
외국어 = foreign language
배우다 = to learn; to study
잘하다 = to be good at something

expressions.korean: 제가 좋아하는 가수들이 다음 달에 콘서트를 할 거예요.
expressions.english: The singers that I like are going to have a concert next month.
expressions.notes:
가수 = singer
다음 = next
콘서트 = concert

expressions.korean: 스포츠를 하는 건 안 좋아하는데 보는 건 정말 좋아해요.
expressions.english: I don't like playing sports, but I really like watching them.
expressions.notes:
스포츠 = sport(s)
스포츠를 하다 = to play sports
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up

expressions.korean: 효진 씨가 좋아할 줄 알았는데 별로 안 좋아하는 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I thought Hyojin would like it, but I don't think she likes it very much.
expressions.notes: -(으)은 줄 알다 = to know how to + V

expressions.korean: 제 친구들은 술 마시는 걸 좋아하는데 자는 별로 안 좋아해요.
expressions.english: My friends like to drink, but I don't really like it.

expressions.korean: 저는 보통 학생들과 다르게 학창 시절 과목 중에 수학을 가장 좋아했었어요.
expressions.english: Unlike normal students, I liked math the most out of all the subjects when I was in school.
expressions.notes:
보통 = usually; nor-mally; usual; normal; regular
학생 = student
다르다 = to be dif-ferent
다르게 is an adverb form of 다르다.
학창 시절 = one's school days
과목 = subject (of study)
수학 = math
가장 = the most
